title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 40.435 - Complaints against commanding officer, procedure
text:
40.435.  Complaints against commanding officer, procedure. — Any member of the state military forces who believes himself wronged by the member's commanding officer, and who, upon due application to that commanding officer, is refused redress, may complain to any superior commissioned officer, who shall forward the complaint to the governor or adjutant general, who shall examine such complaint and take proper measures for redressing the wrong.
(L. 1984 H.B. 1035 § 125)
